summary: Feedback loop strength is driven by direct intervention while execution follow-through remains the main limiter; reliability is 51% with founder truth 59%.

signals.label: Acceptance
signals.note: 0 approvals and 0 assignments across 0 explicit founder events.

signals.label: Feedback Linkage
signals.note: 3 of 8 events link to a concrete intervention.

signals.label: Direct Intervention
signals.note: 8 events are strongly linked to a concrete intervention or action.

signals.label: Rejection Pressure
signals.note: 0 rejection event(s) are linked across the founder feedback stream.

recommendations: Prefer explicit founder-feedback event ingestion over markdown-only recovery whenever approval or override state matters.
Use founder-truth confidence to cap assertiveness when the decision stream is thin or pending-heavy.
Keep increasing intervention linkage so founder feedback can tune strategy and execution policy directly.
Require every executed action to produce an observed outcome, not just a receipt.
Promote recurring pending-approval pressure into explicit product or workflow backlog items.
